corpus.title: News: Harborview City Council — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: Harborview City Council voted to rezone 140 acres of former industrial land as mixed-use development...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Harborview City Council makes a significant move amid changes in the municipal sector

Harborview City Council voted to rezone 140 acres of former industrial land as mixed-use development, expected to add 2,400 housing units by 2027. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in municipal strategy. A spokesperson for Harborview City Council declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — Harborview City Council
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of Harborview City Council data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
EASTPORT CORRIDOR MIXED-USE IMPACT ANALYSIS — FEBRUARY 2024

Site area: 140 acres | Proposed district: MX-3
Projected new housing units: 2,400 (1,920 market-rate, 480 affordable)
Projected population addition: ~4,200 residents
Traffic impact: +18% AM peak hour volume on Dock Street corridor.
Transit mitigation recommended: Bus Rapid Transit extension (cost: $34M, subject to separate Council vote).
Environmental: Former petroleum storage — Remediation Order ER-2021-44 requires soil and groundwater certification before building permits.

corpus.title: Harborview City Council — Ordinance 2024-18: Eastport Industrial Rezoning
corpus.source: Harborview Office of the City Clerk
corpus.snippet: Harborview City Council voted to rezone 140 acres of former industrial land as mixed-use development, expected to add 2,...
corpus.content:
HARBORVIEW CITY COUNCIL — ORDINANCE 2024-18
Adopted: March 11, 2024 | Public Hearing: February 26, 2024

SUBJECT: Rezoning of Eastport Industrial Corridor to Mixed-Use District MX-3

WHEREAS the Eastport Industrial Corridor (140 acres, bounded by Dock Street, Terminal Avenue, and the Harborview Shipping Canal) has remained underutilised since closure of the Consolidated Freight terminal in 2019;

NOW THEREFORE the City Council hereby rezones said 140 acres from Industrial Heavy (IH) to Mixed-Use District MX-3, permitting residential, retail, and light commercial development.

Development projections per Planning Department Impact Analysis (Feb 2024):
  Residential units (projected): 2,400 (mix of market-rate and affordable)
  Retail/commercial floor area: 180,000 sq ft
  Target completion: Phase 1 by Q4 2025; full build-out by end of 2027

Affordable housing requirement: 20% of units at or below 80% AMI.
Environmental remediation cost-sharing: developer-funded ($12M bond required).
VOTE: 6-1 in favour. Councillor Obata dissenting (traffic impact concerns).
